violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC spread out on sheet pan. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
Inspector observed case of ramen noodles in the basement area with rodent gnaw markings. PIC discarded case of noodles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Various items were observed held between 45-79 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded items or placed items in walk-in cooler for active cooling based on time/temp. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Fried chicken was placed hot in the walk-in cooler stacked greater than four inches deep on sheet trays. PIC placed divided chicken up so it was less than 4 inches deep. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The presence of rodents and flies are not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings, gnaw marks, bedding material, and live flies. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The boiler room AND dry storage in the basement areas have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and old droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The following was observed in poor repair. - kitchen floor tiles missing near basement door w/ exposed wood. - basement wall next to stairs. - floors in the basement and walk-in coolers need re-sealing. - leak in basement ceiling with stagnant water on basement floor. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025
The floor and wall junctures in the basement do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
Several hand-washing sinks throughout the establishment were lacking proper hand-washing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Raw animal foods (such as raw eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce and kimchi) in the walk-in refrigerator AND prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC reorganized coolers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (food chopper) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed item in 3 bay sink to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 25 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment to use 3 bay sink for sanitizing until dishwasher has been serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Tuna, beef, batter, and chicken were observed held at between 45-73 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC relocated beef, batter, and tuna and discarded chicken based on temp/time. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Sanitizer was stored above food on the hot line. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. PIC relocated spay bottle. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed pans of hot chicken cooling in the walk-in cooler in containers greater than four inches deep with lids. PIC placed items on sheet pans in walk-in cooler without cover for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The presence of rodents and flies are not controlled as evidenced by large droppings and live flies. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC removed and washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Walk-in meat cooler AND walk-in freezer located in the basement area were not in good repair. Walk-in meat cooler ambient was 70 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC discarded TCS items. Walk-in freezer was observed with an ambient of 62 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC relocated freezer items to freezer chest and produce walk-in cooler. PIC called for service. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
Inspector observed hot water handle at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen area in poor repair (hot water is non-operational). New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: - ceiling tile above dish-machine (missing) - kitchen floor tiles (missing near basement door w/ exposed wooden floor boards) - basement wall near basement stairs (hole) - wall near back kitchen door (hole) - walk-in cooler (FRP panel drooping with exposed wood)The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The boiler room has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025
